detective_persona.name: Det. Harlan Torres
detective_persona.annotation_voice: Terse, systems-minded notes; underlines timestamps and access-control terms; uses arrows to connect doc_ids.

A1.emotional_beat: A slow unease settles in when the memo calls Mara Ellison’s death a likely ‘accidental fall’ yet confidently notes ‘no active oxygen alarm pages’ in Stairwell 4B—an oddly tidy detail for a badge-locked cGMP facility.

detective_annotations.content: ‘DISCOVERY TIME: 06:14’ + ‘Stairwell 4B (outside cGMP)’ — lock this location.
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital yellow highlight with a thin black outline

detective_annotations.content: Facilities says ‘no O₂ alarm pages’… does that mean nothing happened, or that paging was OFF? Ask Briggs (POI-04).
detective_annotations.style_hint: typed callout box, small sans-serif, slightly angled

detective_annotations.content: (Maybe just a missed page / system glitch?) Don’t assume intent yet — but a ‘quiet’ stairwell death in Building 7 feels wrong.
detective_annotations.style_hint: pale-blue digital sticky, skeptical tone

A1.reading_order_note: Read first. This is the ‘official accident’ baseline that later documents (especially C1/C2) will undercut.

A2.emotional_beat: The case snaps into focus as a suspect map: four living POIs with overlapping pressure and permissions, and the realization that ‘QA sign-off’ (Raina Patel) is as dangerous a lever as security access or money.

detective_annotations.content: QA SIGN-OFF PATH → Patel (POI-05). Deviation ownership + release authority = leverage.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin red digital circle with a small arrow

detective_annotations.content: Kline (POI-02): money + deal pressure. Reyes (POI-03): access + fear. Briggs (POI-04): alarms/logs. Patel (POI-05): audit trail. Where do pressure + permissions overlap?
detective_annotations.style_hint: clean gray callout box with bullet-like line breaks

A2.reading_order_note: Best read immediately after A1 so names (Ellison, Kline, Reyes, Briggs, Patel) stick before you hit the noisier public/OSINT material in A3.

A3.emotional_beat: The ‘miracle cure’ hype turns sour when a semi-insider voice drops specific language—‘QA deviation’ and ‘cleaning PDFs since January’—making it feel like the real crime might be paperwork first, violence second.

detective_annotations.content: @bench2board: ‘QA logged a deviation’ + ‘cleaning PDFs since January’ — too specific for random trolling.
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital neon highlight (cyan) on dark-mode UI

detective_annotations.content: If this is real, what’s the deviation ID? DEV-??? Request internal email exports / eQMS history.
detective_annotations.style_hint: small red question mark icon + short callout

A3.reading_order_note: Read before A5 so the Ledger’s polished language lands as a contrast to the rumor of ‘cleaned’ documentation.

A4.emotional_beat: Handling the floor plan makes the death feel engineered: Stairwell 4B isn’t just a place—it’s a controllable pocket with a keyed Utility Closet U-4B, an O₂ monitor, and a camera blind spot big enough to hide a decision.

detective_annotations.content: Blind spot at landing (CAM-4B-LND) — whoever picked 4B knew this gap.
detective_annotations.style_hint: red digital circle + dashed outline

detective_annotations.content: O₂ Monitor ↔ Utility Closet U-4B (KEYED) — access list? key control logs?
detective_annotations.style_hint: straight red arrow with a boxed label

detective_annotations.content: ‘SP-4B’ / ‘Paging relay panel’ mentioned — match exact device names if/when we pull system history.
detective_annotations.style_hint: compact digital annotation, monospace for device IDs

A4.reading_order_note: Read after A1 (so Stairwell 4B matters) and before any deep log documents later; this is the spatial key that makes timestamps feel physically plausible.

A5.emotional_beat: The business press optimism becomes eerie because it’s timestamped (2026-03-20) and full of careful compliance phrasing—especially Kline’s line about ‘any deviation is assessed and documented’—words that feel like a pre-written defense once you remember Ellison is dead by morning.

detective_annotations.content: Printed 2026-03-20 09:10 — THIS is the narrative Helixure needed to protect.
detective_annotations.style_hint: yellow highlight + thin black underline

detective_annotations.content: Kline quote: ‘any deviation is assessed and documented’ — flag this. If docs were ‘cleaned,’ the lie is in what’s missing, not what’s said.
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital callout box with a small ‘!’ tag

B1.emotional_beat: A tight, irritated unease as Evan Kline sounds rehearsed—then the ‘EXEC-0441’ slip at 22:39 makes it feel like he’s been thinking about that exact record all day.

detective_annotations.content: ‘EXEC-0441’ + ‘22:39’ volunteered BEFORE exhibits shown — why does Kline know the label?
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital yellow highlight with a crisp callout

detective_annotations.content: He anchors to 20:03 call → ‘straight home.’ But keeps circling back to access policy. Cross-check B5 for 22:39 event details.
detective_annotations.style_hint: sans-serif note, slightly compressed like typed-in haste

detective_annotations.content: (Maybe wrong) If EXEC-0441 hit is real access, was Kline physically in Bldg 7 at 22:39? Don’t assume—verify.
detective_annotations.style_hint: small blue question icon + short note

B1.reading_order_note: Pairs best BEFORE B5: the impact is feeling sure Kline lied—then letting B5 complicate what ‘EXEC-0441’ actually proves.

B2.emotional_beat: A tug of empathy that curdles into suspicion when Nadia Reyes insists ‘badge-only’ at 21:55—then admits executive escort piggybacking makes the whole building timeline slippery.

detective_annotations.content: ‘Left at 21:55’ — lock this. Compare against any movement after 22:39 in B5.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin red digital circle

detective_annotations.content: Key admission: ‘unless someone’s using executive escort… people slip through.’ That’s how you get bodies without badge hits.
detective_annotations.style_hint: grey callout box, understated

B2.reading_order_note: Read after B1 so ‘executive escort’ feels like a second witness independently describing the same loophole.

B3.emotional_beat: A creeping dread as Tom Briggs casually explains that ‘no page’ can be manufactured with a MAINT/SILENCED state—turning A1’s reassuring ‘no alarms’ line into something weaponizable.

detective_annotations.content: BRIGGS: ‘If it’s in maintenance, it won’t page me.’ This makes A1 ‘no alarm pages’ meaningless.
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital neon highlight + bolded excerpt

detective_annotations.content: Connect: ‘maintenance/silenced’ (B3) → verify actual state in system logs later (C2).
detective_annotations.style_hint: straight blue arrow with small label

detective_annotations.content: Who can set Stairwell 4B monitor to MAINT? Facilities only—or does QA/EHS admin have access too?
detective_annotations.style_hint: compact question badge

B3.reading_order_note: Works best right before B8 so the player recognizes ‘MAINT’ as a loaded word when it appears on the story capture.

B4.emotional_beat: A cold, controlled tension: Raina Patel’s 19:05 alibi sounds immaculate, but her fixation on ‘audit trails’ and ‘exports’ makes it feel like she’s negotiating evidence, not telling truth.

detective_annotations.content: Patel locks in: ‘Left at 19:05.’ Circle this—anything timestamped after-hours breaks her clean story.
detective_annotations.style_hint: pale yellow highlight + small clock icon

detective_annotations.content: She keeps steering to ‘authenticated session’ + ‘export’ language. Feels preemptive. Compare with B7 (review email) + later system export evidence.
detective_annotations.style_hint: tight sans-serif note in a light-blue callout

B4.reading_order_note: Read after B7 if possible; Patel’s evasions land harder once the DEV-102-17 request is fresh in mind.

B5.emotional_beat: An ‘oh no’ realization: the boring Lenel export doesn’t name a killer—it proves the building can be navigated on shared credentials and escort exceptions, meaning every confident alibi suddenly needs rechecking.

detective_annotations.content: 22:39:03 ACCESS GRANTED — CRED: EXEC-0441 (shared). This resolves CONTRA-01 but does NOT equal Kline.
detective_annotations.style_hint: red circle + bolded time

detective_annotations.content: ‘ESCORT MODE ENABLED’ + Host badge: QA-ADMIN-7719 (15 min). This matches Reyes’s ‘people slip through’ (B2).
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital yellow highlight with a pinned callout

detective_annotations.content: Look for any reader/location strings near Stairwell 4B / L4 corridor. Tie to A4 route + B6 cell sector STW4B.
detective_annotations.style_hint: small grey note like an analyst sidebar

B5.reading_order_note: This doc should land after B1 + B2 so it feels like a forensic ‘reality check’ on both interviews’ claims.

B6.emotional_beat: A tightening, claustrophobic urgency as Ellison’s last outgoing SMS at 23:11:52 (Sector: STW4B) is followed by a wall of missed calls—like a signal cutting off mid-sentence.

detective_annotations.content: 23:11:52 OUT — ‘Heading to the garage’ — Cell: HLX-B7-FEMTO-L4 (Sector: STW4B). This is minutes before 23:12.
detective_annotations.style_hint: bright highlight + digital sticky marker

detective_annotations.content: 22:06 SMS w/ ‘R. Patel’ re: ‘deviation folder’ → motive pressure. Pair with B7 DEV-102-17 language.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin arrow with tiny label tag

detective_annotations.content: First ‘NO ANSWER/VM’ after 23:12 — treat as incapacitation window marker, not a random missed call.
detective_annotations.style_hint: compact note in a white callout box

B6.reading_order_note: Place before Envelope C: it primes the player to care about the 23:12 minute and recognize it when system logs appear.

B7.emotional_beat: A slow burn of anger and dread as Ellison demands raw audit-trail access (DEV-102-17 / Batch 14C) while Kline pushes to ‘take it off email’—and Patel offers only a curated ‘regulator-facing PDF package.’

detective_annotations.content: ELLISON 18:30 — ‘full DEV-102-17 folder’ + ‘raw audit-trail export’ + ‘before Monday 09:00.’ This is the fuse.
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital highlight with underlined deviation ID

detective_annotations.content: Kline after the 20:03 call: ‘off email’ pressure = fear of discoverability. Matches his tone in B1.
detective_annotations.style_hint: light-blue callout, clean sans-serif

detective_annotations.content: Patel offers ‘regulator-facing PDF package’ (curated). Compare to her 19:05 alibi claims in B4.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin red circle + small ‘!’ in the margin

B7.reading_order_note: Read after B4 if possible: Patel’s interview becomes immediately suspect once you’ve seen how she frames ‘exports’ and ‘regulator-facing’ packages here.

B8.emotional_beat: A prickling sense of being watched as a jokey Instagram story places someone at Safety Panel SP-4B at 22:50 with a visible ‘MAINT’ banner—then the post vanishes by morning.

detective_annotations.content: METADATA: 2026-03-20 22:50 + label ‘SP-4B’ + banner ‘MAINT.’ That’s inside the critical period.
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital highlighter with a small pin icon

detective_annotations.content: Overlay: ‘night audit — don’t page Facilities’ → sounds like the exact ‘maintenance/silenced’ trick Briggs described in B3. Who posted, and why delete by ~07:30?
detective_annotations.style_hint: compact question callout with muted purple border

B8.reading_order_note: Best after B3 so ‘MAINT’ reads as a functional system state, not just a vague caption.

C1.emotional_beat: A cold snap of certainty: this wasn’t a bad fall—Ellison was deprived of oxygen first, and the ‘accident’ story collapses into something planned.

detective_annotations.content: Hypoxia BEFORE impact — this flips A1’s ‘accident’ assumption.
detective_annotations.style_hint: yellow digital highlight with a tight callout box

detective_annotations.content: Time window ~23:10–23:30. Compare to B6 last activity + any system events.
detective_annotations.style_hint: red circle; underlined twice

detective_annotations.content: Bruising on both upper arms = handled/dragged? Staging after collapse.
detective_annotations.style_hint: small sans-serif note; clipped, matter-of-fact

C1.reading_order_note: Best read immediately before C2 so the player is primed to interpret ‘maintenance/silence’ and purge events as a homicide method, not a safety glitch.

C2.emotional_beat: An exacting, stomach-dropping aha: the timeline is engineered—silenced monitor, purge event, then a tidy export—each timestamp tightening the trap.

detective_annotations.content: 22:48 MAINT/SILENCE → paging disabled. This is the answer to ‘my phone never rang’ (B3).
detective_annotations.style_hint: cyan highlight with a rectangular callout

detective_annotations.content: 23:12 purge → O2 drop → aligns with C1 window. Method, not accident.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin black arrow; timestamp tags

detective_annotations.content: Could ‘MAINT’ be a routine nightly test? If so, why paired with a purge + late export?
detective_annotations.style_hint: small gray note; intentionally skeptical

detective_annotations.content: DR node / checksum = harder to tamper with. Treat as ‘source of truth.’
detective_annotations.style_hint: tight circle; bolded words

C2.reading_order_note: Read before C3: C2 establishes the raw, append-only ‘what happened’ record; C3 then shows the polished narrative someone tried to publish on top of it.

C3.emotional_beat: Rage and disbelief: someone generated a clean, regulator-friendly ‘sensor fault’ story minutes after the dangerous window—before anyone ‘found’ the body.

detective_annotations.content: Generated at 23:36… same night. Why writing the story BEFORE discovery at 06:14 (A1)?
detective_annotations.style_hint: pink highlight with a bold timestamp callout

detective_annotations.content: ‘Transient sensor fault’ — but C2 shows a purge + O2 drop. This reads like damage control.
detective_annotations.style_hint: red circle with a short side-note

detective_annotations.content: Generated by account label ≠ person. Confirm who ‘RPATEL (QA-DIR)’ maps to + whether it’s shared.
detective_annotations.style_hint: clean sans-serif note; cautious phrasing

C3.reading_order_note: Works best as the last C doc: after the player trusts C2’s raw log, this ‘print to PDF’ summary feels like a deliberate attempt to overwrite reality.

R1.emotional_beat: A sober click of certainty: the ‘accident’ framing finally dies when the 23:12 nitrogen purge and pre-impact hypoxia line up on one timeline.

detective_annotations.content: This sentence ends the ‘routine fall’ theory from A1 — officially homicide now.
detective_annotations.style_hint: yellow highlight with a tight digital callout box

detective_annotations.content: Anchor time: 23:12 purge (C2). Use this to sanity-check every alibi claim.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin red circle; timestamp underlined twice

R1.reading_order_note: Read this first in Envelope R. It sets the final, evidence-anchored timeline (C1 + C2) before you evaluate the POI disposition memos.

R2.emotional_beat: Relief mixed with irritation: Kline had the sleazy motive and corporate evasions, but the method evidence won’t stay pinned to him once the shared credential loophole is stated plainly.

detective_annotations.content: Key takeaway: EXEC-0441 is a record, not a person. Shared credential ≠ physical presence.
detective_annotations.style_hint: blue highlight; clean sans-serif callout

detective_annotations.content: Still… his ‘EXEC-0441’ slip in B1 felt too fast. Not murder—maybe optics/obstruction.
detective_annotations.style_hint: small gray text box; cautious tone

R2.reading_order_note: This plays best after R1. The emotional release depends on the player remembering how suspicious B1 felt before B5/C2 reframed it.

R3.emotional_beat: A gentler kind of closure: Reyes is guilty of fear-driven ‘paper sins,’ but the 22:48–23:17 engineered window makes it clear she’s not the one who turned the building into a weapon.

detective_annotations.content: ‘Paper liar’ vs. murderer — her audit panic explains B2, not C2.
detective_annotations.style_hint: yellow highlight; short note pinned to the margin

detective_annotations.content: Critical window called out again: 22:48 (silence) → 23:12 (purge) → ~23:17 (collapse).
detective_annotations.style_hint: tight circle with an arrow pointing to ‘engineered-event window’

R3.reading_order_note: Read after R2 if you want a clean ‘suspect triage’ feeling: finance motive cleared (R2), then messy-but-human lab motive cleared (R3).

R4.emotional_beat: Uncomfortable accountability: Briggs isn’t the killer, but his ‘this is normal’ security culture reads like the open door the offender walked through.

detective_annotations.content: ‘Phone never rang’ (B3) isn’t an alibi when MAINT/SILENT exists (C2 @ 22:48).
detective_annotations.style_hint: orange highlight; bolded timestamp

detective_annotations.content: Policy fix: escort/override after-hours needs written approval + auto-expire. B5 shouldn’t be this ambiguous.
detective_annotations.style_hint: digital sticky note; ‘Action Item’ label

R4.reading_order_note: This memo lands hardest if read right before R5: it clarifies how the system could be exploited, then R5 shows who did exploit it.

R5.emotional_beat: Catharsis with a chill: the motive (DEV-102-17 and audit-trail exposure) and the method (silence + purge + export) converge into a timestamp chain that feels too engineered to deny.

detective_annotations.content: Four timestamps, one narrative: 22:39 (return) → 22:48 (silence) → 23:12 (purge) → 23:36 (export).
detective_annotations.style_hint: green highlight; bracketed timeline in the margin

detective_annotations.content: B7 (DEV-102-17 panic) → C2/C3 (RPATEL session) = motive meets method.
detective_annotations.style_hint: thin black arrow with labeled endpoints

detective_annotations.content: The cover-story move is the 23:36 ‘sensor fault’ export. That’s consciousness of guilt, not confusion.
detective_annotations.style_hint: clean digital callout; ‘why it matters’ tone

R5.reading_order_note: Read this last in Envelope R. The payoff depends on having R1’s homicide determination and the cleared-POI logic (R2–R4) already in your head.

trojan_horse_verification.appears_as: A routine entry/exit and badge-access log export collected ‘for completeness.’
trojan_horse_verification.actually_proves: A specific after-hours access exception (escort/override behavior) aligns with the murder window, showing the killer relied on procedural loopholes to obscure identity.

trojan_horse_verification.appears_as: A boring carrier-style calls/SMS log from Ellison’s phone—mostly verification codes and mundane contacts.
trojan_horse_verification.actually_proves: Ellison’s last authenticated activity and phone movement patterns tighten the time-of-incapacitation and contradict assumptions about when she ‘fell.’
